last_item: pgbouncer Reload never verified the pooler was still running after a reload/restart (band-1 correctness) — contradicting DEFAULTS.md 'verify it's still running after'; a SIGHUP reload can exit 0 while PgBouncer dies parsing a bad config, so Reload reported success over a dead pooler
last_result: SHIPPED: (*Manager).Reload (internal/pgbouncer/service.go) now verifies PgBouncer is actually active after the reload — or its restart fallback — exits 0, instead of returning nil the instant systemctl exited 0. After a 0-exit it calls IsRunning and: propagates the undeterminable-state error ('couldn't ask systemctl'), returns a loud core.ExecError + hint (check status/logs, restore previous config) when the pooler is down, or logs success. A reload that exits 0 but leaves the pooler dead errors immediately (no restart of the same rejected config, no needless connection drop). CodeExec matches the codebase convention for 'service didn't come up after a systemd op' (safeconfig.go:151, upgrade.go:235). Only caller is Enable; its own later IsRunning gate is preserved for the no-change path. Test-first + mutation-proven (inverted-condition `if running` mutation reds the guards, then reverted): updated the 2 existing Reload tests to register a healthy is-active + pin the verify call; added TestReload_ErrorsWhenPoolerDeadAfterReload / _DeadAfterRestart / _RunStateUndeterminableAfterApply; updated TestEnable_ServiceNotRunningAfterStartIsNotRecorded (CodeInternal->CodeExec, caught earlier in Reload). code-reviewer clean; test-skeptic found the 2 new dead-pooler tests pinned call positions but not count (trailing-extra-call escape) -> closed with require.Len bounds. Gates green (gofmt/vet/test/build); web untouched; e2e Docker-heavy + not needed for this unit fix.
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
